name: jetson-edgellm-v080
description: v0.8.0 migrated edgellm stack on Jetson Orin NX: Qwen3 ASR + Qwen3 TTS via the v0.8.0 serving worker binaries (one-shot, generic-runner). Engine paths come from the image-baked EDGE_LLM_*_DIR env (deploy_paths source of truth) pointing at the runtime-pulled /opt/edgellm-v080/engines tree. The v0.8.0 qwen3_tts_worker is the GENERIC LLMEngineRunner: it accepts ONLY --talkerEngineDir/--codePredictorEngineDir/--tokenizerDir/--code2wavEngineDir, so the explicit-KV keys (EDGE_LLM_TTS_TALKER_BACKEND/TALKER_ENGINE/CODE_PREDICTOR_BACKEND/TEXT_PROJECTION/PROMPT_KV_CACHE) are intentionally OMITTED (all-empty → no flags) and OVS_TTS_WORKER_CONCURRENCY is 1 (one-shot worker reports max_slots:1, so --max_slots is never emitted). ASR runs EDGE_LLM_ASR_STREAM_MODE=accumulate (v080-0018): the v0.8.0 qwen3_asr_worker implements ONLY the one-shot {requests:[...]} request shape (streaming begin/chunk/end return 501 stub — Option A port deferred). accumulate routes create_stream() to _TRTEdgeLLMAccumulatingASRStream, which accumulates PCM in Python and runs the proven one-shot worker path (_transcribe_worker) on finalize — the worker 'begin' is never sent, so no 501. The stream chunk/mel keys below are unused in accumulate mode (kept for an eventual streaming-worker port) and the Python-side mel (audio_bytes_to_mel) is used instead of worker PCM mel.
